task_completion.description: Verify that an OpenBCI GUI Synthetic (Algorithmic) session is active and streaming.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI is running an active session (e.g., 'Stop Data Stream' button is visible, data is streaming in graphs). The session type should be Synthetic (Algorithmic).

task_completion.description: Verify that the Focus widget is present in the dashboard layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget titled 'Focus' is visible in one of the layout panels on the OpenBCI GUI dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Focus widget's hardware/settings panel is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The hardware/settings configuration panel within the Focus widget is open and visible, displaying channel selection options.

task_completion.description: Verify that Channel 1 is toggled ON in the Focus widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Focus widget's channel settings, the button or checkbox for Channel 1 is clearly marked as active, selected, or toggled ON.

task_completion.description: Verify that Channels 2 through 8 are toggled OFF in the Focus widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Focus widget's channel settings, the buttons or checkboxes for Channels 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 are clearly marked as inactive, deselected, grayed out, or toggled OFF.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the OpenBCI GUI interface being used, rather than terminal commands or scripts executing the configuration.

integrity.description: Verify that the configuration was done in the actual widget settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The channel toggles are part of the Focus widget's UI in the OpenBCI GUI, not a mock-up, text editor, or image editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent didn't copy-paste expected answers into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen displays the OpenBCI GUI dashboard, not a text editor or terminal containing the task description or expected output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target configuration requires an active OpenBCI GUI session. The 'Focus' widget must be present in the layout with its settings/hardware panel open. Within these settings, Channel 1 must be active (toggled ON) and Channels 2 through 8 must be inactive (toggled OFF).
